laws.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 repeals 28 U.S.C. §§ 353, 359, and 361 and replaces chapter 16 part I with a new Supreme Court complaints-and-discipline framework. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 supplies the short title, and Section 2 states the purpose is to transfer judicial misconduct complaint authority to the Supreme Court.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 28 U.S.C. §§ 353, 359, and 361 and rewrites chapter 16 part I.
provisions.exact_change: Strike §§ 353, 359, and 361, then replace chapter 16 part I with the enacted complaints-and-discipline text.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(1) says those sections "shall be repealed," and Section 3(2) says chapter 16 part I "shall be amended to read as follows."
provisions.notes: The source is a chapter-level rewrite, not a freestanding note.

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 preserves the remainder of the Act if any provision is held invalid.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 makes the Act effective immediately upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is parked at Title 28 section 1 and carries a quoted-content dump, but the required rewrite is the chapter 16 complaint and discipline text.

recommended_actions: Apply the chapter 16 rewrite in the affected Title 28 text.
Strike the three repealed sections and replace chapter 16 part I with the enacted Supreme Court framework.
Reduce the note to concise history and remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-244 | Sentencing Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends Pub. L. No. 29-207 §§ 7(b) and 7(c), and Section 4 amends 18 U.S.C. § 3585. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 names the Act and Section 2 says it reforms sentencing in the United States.

provisions.text_summary: Caps maximum sentences under Pub. L. No. 29-207 §§ 7(b) and 7(c).
provisions.exact_change: Replace the existing maximum-sentence rules in §§ 7(b) and 7(c) with the enacted caps for death/life offenses and term-of-years offenses.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(a) and 3(b) say the two subsections of Pub. L. No. 29-207 "shall be amended to read" the new maximum-sentence rules.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 18 U.S.C. § 3585 on commencement of sentence and prior-custody credit.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the current commencement and custody-credit text in § 3585 with the enacted wording.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(a) states that 18 U.S.C. § 3585 "shall be amended to read as follows."

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 preserves the Act if any part fails, and Section 6 makes it effective on enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is anchored at Title 18 section 1, but the source requires a rewrite of Pub. L. No. 29-207 sections 7(b) and 7(c) plus 18 U.S.C. § 3585.

recommended_actions: Apply the Pub. L. No. 29-207 sentencing changes and the 18 U.S.C. § 3585 rewrite.
Do not leave the operative changes as note-only history.
Trim the note and remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-245 | Legal Compensation Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d), and Section 4 repeals 42 U.S.C. chapter 34 subchapter X while creating a new grant-support framework. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 names the Act, and Section 2 says it supports legal representation and compensation.

provisions.text_summary: Amends the payment rules in 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d).
provisions.exact_change: Replace the compensation text in subsection (d) with the enacted payment framework.
provisions.evidence: Section 3 says 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d) "shall be amended to read as follows."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 42 U.S.C. chapter 34 subchapter X and creates Judicial Conference grant authority, with relationship-to-other-laws language.
provisions.exact_change: Remove the repealed subchapter and preserve the new grant-support authority as note material.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(a) repeals the subchapter, and Section 4(b) authorizes the Judicial Conference to administer grants and rewards for legal representation support.
provisions.notes: The new grant program is uncodified note material rather than a positive-law Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 preserves the remainder of the Act if a provision is invalidated.

provisions.text_summary: Effective date.
provisions.evidence: Section 6 makes the Act effective upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is on a Title 18 anchor, but it does not perform the required 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d) rewrite.

recommended_actions: Apply the 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d) text replacement.
Preserve the repealed subchapter and the grant-support program only as concise note history.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-246 | Sentencing Commission Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3(a) repeals 28 U.S.C. §§ 992, 994(p-s,w-x), and 995(a)(2-3); Section 3(b) rewrites § 991(a); and Section 3(c) adds new approval and responsibilities text to § 994. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 names the Act and Section 2 states it reforms the Sentencing Commission and transfers its authorities.

provisions.exact_change: Mark the listed portions of §§ 992, 994, and 995 for repeal.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(a) says the listed 28 U.S.C. provisions "shall be repealed in their entirety."

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 28 U.S.C. § 991(a) to place the Commission under the Judicial Conference.
provisions.exact_change: Replace subsection (a) with the new Commission structure, membership, and removal rules.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(b) says 28 U.S.C. § 991(a) "shall be amended to read as follows."

provisions.text_summary: Adds Judicial Conference approval and new responsibilities to 28 U.S.C. § 994.
provisions.exact_change: Append the Judicial Conference approval requirement and the listed additional responsibilities to § 994.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(c) says § 994 "shall be amended by adding at the end the following."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 preserves the rest of the Act if any provision fails.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note sits at Title 28 section 1, but the operative changes belong in §§ 991, 992, 994, and 995.

recommended_actions: Apply the Title 28 structural rewrite in the affected sections.
Mark the listed repeal portions and replace §§ 991(a) and 994 with the enacted text.
Keep the remaining material as concise note history and remove the dump/Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-247 | Grand Jury Bypass Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It creates a generally applicable administrative-subpoena and nondisclosure framework but does not supply a positive-law U.S. Code rewrite target, so it belongs in statutory-note form at the closest subject-matter anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: Sections 1 through 3 only set the short title, severability, and immediate effective date.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for recipients and the Attorney General's designee.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 defines "the recipient" and "his designee" for the subpoena framework.
provisions.notes: No positive-law Code host is identified.

provisions.text_summary: Authorizes Attorney General subpoenas, service, enforcement, and court-compelled compliance.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 authorizes written subpoenas, service, court enforcement, and contempt remedies for noncompliance.

provisions.text_summary: Challenges to subpoenas and protection from self-incrimination.
provisions.evidence: Section 6 provides a motion-to-modify process and addresses self-incrimination limits on testimony demands.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the correct Title 28 neighborhood, but it still carries a full-text dump and Trello link instead of a concise history note.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as a statutory note only.
Do not fabricate a positive-law Code section for the subpoena framework.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-248 | Intelligence Community Inspector General Consolidation Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It consolidates Intelligence Community Inspector General functions and adjusts oversight rules, but the enactment is best preserved as a statutory note anchored to Title 50 rather than as a clean positive-law rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 provides the short title, and Section 2 defines the IGIC, intelligence community element, element IG, and designation authority.

provisions.text_summary: Transfers element IG duties, personnel, records, property, and pending matters to the IGIC.
provisions.evidence: Sections 3 and 4 move duties, employees, records, property, and pending matters into the IGIC structure.
provisions.notes: The operative text is organizational rather than a line-by-line U.S. Code substitution.

provisions.text_summary: Limits IGIC access to sensitive operations and sets written justification rules.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 lets the DNI restrict direct access for national-security reasons and requires classified written justification.

provisions.text_summary: Protects intelligence sources and methods.
provisions.evidence: Section 7 preserves the DNI's duty to protect intelligence sources and methods from unauthorized disclosure.

provisions.text_summary: Additional oversight powers and effective date.
provisions.evidence: Section 8 adds oversight-related duties and penalties, and Section 9 makes the Act effective three days after enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is on a Title 50 anchor and matches the subject matter, but it still needs to be reduced to a concise statutory note.

recommended_actions: Keep the law as a statutory note at the Title 50 anchor.
Do not invent a positive-law rewrite for the IGIC consolidation scheme.
Trim the quoted-content dump and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-249 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Bill 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It is a place-specific diplomacy/security enactment with no express positive-law Code amendment, so it belongs in statutory-note form at the closest Title 40 anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 names the Act and Section 2 states its purpose is to establish and define jurisdiction over the Complex.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for the Complex, Department, and United Nations Secretariat.
provisions.evidence: Section 3 defines the Complex, the Department, and the United Nations Secretariat.

provisions.text_summary: Jurisdiction, control, access control, and security authority over the Complex.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 gives the Department full jurisdiction, control, and authority over the Complex and its security perimeter.

provisions.text_summary: United Nations access rules and suspension/revocation conditions.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 allows access, suspension, and permanent revocation rules tied to Department security judgments.

provisions.text_summary: Regulations and severability.
provisions.evidence: Section 6 requires regulations within fourteen days, and Section 7 is severability.

current_implementation.detail: The note is placed at a plausible Title 40 anchor, but the record still contains a quoted-content dump and should be condensed.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as a concise statutory note.
Do not fabricate a positive-law section rewrite.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-250 | Federal Prison Standards Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It creates broad detention-facility standards, property-boundary rules, force rules, oversight, and remedies, but it does not supply a clean positive-law Code rewrite target. It belongs in statutory-note form at the closest Title 18 anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: Sections 1 through 3 establish the name of the Act, its prison-safety purposes, and the core definitions.

provisions.text_summary: Property boundaries, access authority, and restricted airspace for the Washington D.C. Correctional Facility.
provisions.evidence: Sections 4 through 6 set facility boundaries, access rules, and airspace restrictions for the correctional facility.

provisions.text_summary: Standards of care, abuse prohibitions, lethal-force limits, and solitary-confinement rules.
provisions.evidence: Sections 7 through 10 regulate conditions of confinement, force, and solitary confinement.

provisions.text_summary: Oversight, whistleblower protection, regulations, civil remedies, and criminal remedies.
provisions.evidence: Sections 11 and 12 create inspection, reporting, whistleblower, and enforcement provisions, including references to 18 U.S.C. § 113 and 42 U.S.C. § 1983.
provisions.notes: The Act references criminal and civil remedies but does not identify a single clean U.S. Code host for a rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Recognition, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: Section 13 contains a recognition clause, Section 14 is severability, and Section 15 makes the Act effective immediately.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is on a Title 18 anchor and matches the subject matter, but it still contains a full-text dump and should be shortened.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as a statutory note only.
Do not invent a positive-law detention or prison section.
Trim the quoted-content dump and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-251 | In-Game Sentencing Guidelines Bill
laws.status_basis: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as superseded history. It should remain retained only as statutory history rather than live positive text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: Sections 1 through 3 set the bill's title, severability, and effective date.

provisions.text_summary: Defines the Title 18 sentencing framework and aggregate-sentence limits.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 establishes the sentencing caps and aggregate-sentence limitations for the in-game framework.
provisions.notes: The enactment is retained only as historical material.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML already treats this law as superseded history, which matches the archive classification; the remaining work is cleanup of the dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep this enactment as historical note material only.
Do not revive it as live positive-law text.
Trim the quoted-content dump and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 35-252 | Magistrate Appointment Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends Section 106(c)(i) of Public Law 26-190, but it does not identify a clean positive-law U.S. Code text to rewrite. The enactment should remain note material at the closest Title 28 anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: Section 1 names the Act and Section 2 states that it changes the magistrate-judge appointment process.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 106(c)(i) of Public Law 26-190.
provisions.exact_change: Update the referenced magistrate-appointment rule in PL 26-190 to require 30 days as a bar-certified individual, with the federal-bar suspension proviso.
provisions.evidence: Section 3 says Section 106(c)(i) of Public Law 26-190 "shall be amended to read as follows."
provisions.notes: This is a prior-public-law amendment rather than a direct U.S. Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 preserves the remainder of the Act if any provision is invalid.

provisions.text_summary: Effective date.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 makes the Act effective upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is at a plausible Title 28 anchor, but the record still carries a full-text dump and should remain as concise note history only.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as note history at the closest Title 28 anchor.
Do not fabricate a positive-law section rewrite.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
